key_highlights: Massive AI-led tech selloff: Nasdaq 100 sinks 5% as AI trade unwinds, with Bitcoin plunging below $60,000 for the first time since October 2024.
Strong U.S. jobs report (172,000 jobs added, unemployment steady at 4.3%) fuels Fed rate hike bets, boosting the dollar and pressuring emerging-market currencies and commodities.
Geopolitical tensions escalate: Hezbollah rejects ceasefire proposal, supporting oil advances; Indonesia faces crisis of confidence as markets decode Prabowo.
Corporate debt restructuring: Raizen inks $13 billion out-of-court debt deal; Austria downgrade ends era in club of Europe’s safest borrowers.
Regulatory and policy shifts: U.S. officials eye government shares in AI giants; Anthropic calls for global pause in AI development; China deepens focus on fund returns.
SpaceX IPO anticipation: SpaceX aims for $1.77 trillion valuation debut, but S&P denies fast index entry; retail investor strength tested amid tech selloff.
Lululemon flubbed quarter, bleak forecasts deepen turnaround worries; Berkshire Hathaway puts $10 billion into Alphabet's AI build-out.
JPMorgan turns rosy on Tesla a day after Dimon lauds Musk; Broadcom shares dip despite AI revenue guidance; Cisco upgraded on anticipated $6 billion in FY2027 AI revenue.
Mortgage rates mixed, high-yield savings up to 4.10% APY; gold sinking as dollar and yields rise.
EVE Online virtual war results in hundreds of thousands of pounds of in-game destruction; flesh-eating screwworm returns to U.S. after 60 years.

sectors.trend: Sharp selloff: Nasdaq 100 sinks 5% in AI-led rout. TSMC signs major AI deal with Nvidia, but Broadcom shares dip despite AI revenue guidance. Applied Materials continues bull run. Cisco upgraded on AI revenue potential. Micron sees over 80% upside from Susquehanna. Berkshire Hathaway puts $10 billion into Alphabet's AI build-out. JPMorgan turns rosy on Tesla.
sectors.implications: Short-term volatility as AI trade unwinds, but long-term AI investment remains strong. Opportunities in semiconductor and AI infrastructure plays. Retail investor strength tested ahead of SpaceX IPO.

sectors.trend: Bitcoin plunges to near $60,000 (first time since Oct 2024), wiping $62 billion from corporate treasury holders. Ethereum also declines. HYPE falls 14%. AI trade unwind and Fed rate hike fears drive selloff.
sectors.implications: High risk of further downside if Fed hikes. MicroStrategy model questioned. Investors should reduce crypto exposure or hedge with options.

sectors.trend: Oil advances as Hezbollah rejects ceasefire proposal, supporting weekly gains. Norway averts offshore strike. Rising crude oil prices becoming a major risk for Indian markets.
sectors.implications: Geopolitical risk premium persists. Oil prices likely to remain elevated, benefiting energy stocks but pressuring import-dependent economies.

sectors.trend: Century Communities announces new projects in Florida, Kentucky. Toll Brothers launches luxury townhomes in Georgia. PulteGroup and Meritage Homes among best real estate stocks. Mortgage rates mixed, HELOC rates move upward. Lululemon flubbed quarter, bleak forecasts deepen turnaround worries.
sectors.implications: Housing demand remains resilient in select markets, but rising rates and inflation pressure affordability. Homebuilders with strong land positions and luxury focus may outperform.

sectors.trend: Emerging-market currencies plunge on blowout US jobs report. Indonesia faces crisis of confidence as markets decode Prabowo. Philippine court drops remaining Marcos ill-gotten wealth case. China, New Zealand hold trade talks. HKMA implements new bank rules.
sectors.implications: Strong dollar and rising US yields pressure EM assets. Indonesia and Philippines face political uncertainty. China trade talks positive but limited impact.

sectors.trend: Lululemon slides on bleak forecasts, deepening turnaround worries. GM CEO Mary Barra discusses business in America. Jozy Altidore bullish on US World Cup run. Americans love subscriptions but 30% spend more than they realize.
sectors.implications: Consumer spending resilient but inflation squeezing budgets. Lululemon's challenges highlight retail headwinds. Subscription economy still growing but consumer fatigue risk.

sectors.trend: JPMorgan taps AI to process checks. Susquehanna building prediction market business. Online betting breached €1 billion in Ireland. Captive equity boom rewrites rules for Europe's CLO and leveraged loan markets. Rosen Law Firm urges GeneDx stockholders to contact firm.
sectors.implications: AI adoption in banking accelerates. Prediction markets and online betting show growth. European leveraged loan market evolving. Legal risks for GeneDx.

sectors.trend: AstraZeneca CEO says AI reshaping drug development. First dual GLP-1/GIP receptor agonist for type 1 diabetes shows promising benefits. GeneDx faces legal scrutiny.
sectors.implications: AI-driven drug discovery gaining traction. GLP-1/GIP agonists expand into type 1 diabetes, opening new market. GeneDx legal risk manageable but watch.

sectors.trend: Applied Materials continues bull run. ASML CEO says EU Commission should not try to direct 'strategic projects'. Marvell Technology, Flex to join S&P 500. Installed Building Products neutral rating reiterated.
sectors.implications: Semiconductor equipment demand strong. EU industrial policy tensions. S&P 500 additions signal quality. IBP neutral suggests caution in building products.

key_themes: AI-led tech selloff and rotation out of growth stocks
Fed rate hike expectations driven by strong jobs data and sticky inflation
Geopolitical risks: Hezbollah ceasefire rejection, Indonesia political uncertainty, US-China trade talks
Corporate debt restructuring and credit market evolution
Regulatory scrutiny on AI development and government stakes in AI giants
SpaceX IPO anticipation and retail investor strength test
Cryptocurrency crash and MicroStrategy model questioned
Oil price rise and impact on import-dependent economies
Consumer spending resilience vs. inflation squeeze
AI adoption across sectors: banking, drug development, check processing

risk_assessment.mitigation: Reduce duration in bond portfolios, favor floating-rate instruments, increase cash allocation. Hedge equity exposure with put options or inverse ETFs.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: AI Trade Unwind / Tech Bubble Burst
risk_assessment.mitigation: Diversify away from pure-play AI names into value and defensive sectors. Consider profit-taking on high-multiple tech stocks. Increase exposure to AI infrastructure plays with strong fundamentals (e.g., TSMC, ASML, Applied Materials).
Increase exposure to energy stocks (oil, defense) and gold. Reduce exposure to EM currencies and equities. Monitor Indonesia policy shifts and US-China trade talks.
Avoid direct crypto exposure. Monitor corporate treasury holdings (MicroStrategy). Consider shorting crypto-related equities or buying puts on Bitcoin futures.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Credit Market Stress (Raizen, Austria downgrade, CLO evolution)
risk_assessment.mitigation: Favor investment-grade bonds over high-yield. Monitor Raizen debt deal and Austria downgrade for contagion. CLO market evolution may create opportunities in senior tranches.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Regulatory Risk (AI pause, government stakes, Paramount deal, China fund overhaul)
risk_assessment.mitigation: Diversify across geographies and sectors. Monitor Anthropic's call for AI pause and US government stake proposals. Paramount deal uncertainty may affect media stocks. China fund overhaul could impact China-exposed funds.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Consumer Spending Slowdown (Lululemon, subscription fatigue, inflation squeeze)
risk_assessment.mitigation: Reduce exposure to discretionary retail. Favor essentials and discount retailers. Monitor subscription economy trends. Lululemon's weakness may signal broader consumer strain.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: SpaceX IPO Disappointment / Retail Investor Weakness
risk_assessment.mitigation: If participating in SpaceX IPO, size position appropriately. Monitor retail investor sentiment and tech selloff impact. S&P denial of fast index entry may dampen enthusiasm.

opportunities.recommendation: Accumulate AI infrastructure leaders on weakness (TSMC, ASML, Applied Materials, Broadcom)
opportunities.rationale: AI capex cycle remains intact despite short-term selloff. TSMC-Nvidia deal, ASML's strategic importance, Applied Materials bull run, and Broadcom's AI revenue guidance support long-term growth. Berkshire Hathaway's $10B investment in Alphabet's AI build-out validates thesis.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy energy stocks on geopolitical risk premium (oil majors, defense)
opportunities.rationale: Hezbollah ceasefire rejection supports oil prices. Norway strike averted but supply risks remain. Oil price rise benefits producers. Defense stocks may benefit from geopolitical tensions.

opportunities.recommendation: Invest in homebuilders with strong land positions and luxury focus (Toll Brothers, Century Communities, PulteGroup)
opportunities.rationale: Housing demand resilient in select markets. Toll Brothers luxury townhomes in Georgia, Century Communities projects in Florida/Kentucky. PulteGroup among best real estate stocks. Rising rates may pressure affordability but luxury segment less sensitive.

opportunities.recommendation: Consider buying the dip in high-quality tech stocks (Microsoft, Alphabet, Apple ahead of WWDC)
opportunities.rationale: Goldman Sachs tech stocks to buy include Microsoft, Alphabet, Nvidia. Morgan Stanley positive ahead of Apple WWDC. Berkshire Hathaway's $10B in Alphabet signals confidence. Tech selloff may be overdone for names with strong fundamentals.

opportunities.recommendation: Explore prediction market and online betting growth (Susquehanna, Ireland betting boom)
opportunities.rationale: Susquehanna building prediction market business. Online betting breached €1 billion in Ireland. Regulatory clarity and technology adoption driving growth. Consider exposure through fintech or gaming ETFs.

opportunities.recommendation: Invest in AI-driven healthcare (AstraZeneca, GLP-1/GIP agonists)
opportunities.rationale: AstraZeneca CEO says AI reshaping drug development. First dual GLP-1/GIP agonist for type 1 diabetes shows promise. AI in biotech reduces failure rates and accelerates timelines.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Increase cash allocation and short-duration bonds
defensive_moves.rationale: Fed rate hike expectations rising. High-yield savings up to 4.10% APY, CDs up to 4% APY. Cash provides optionality to buy dips. Short-duration bonds reduce interest rate risk.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Hedge equity portfolios with put options or inverse ETFs
defensive_moves.rationale: Nasdaq 100 sinking 5%, tech selloff may continue. Bitcoin crash wiping out corporate treasuries. Put options on QQQ or SPY provide downside protection. Inverse ETFs like SQQQ for aggressive hedges.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Reduce exposure to emerging market currencies and equities
defensive_moves.rationale: Strong dollar and rising US yields pressure EM assets. Indonesia crisis of confidence, Philippine court decision, EM currency plunge. Favor developed market exposure.
Bitcoin below $60,000 first time since Oct 2024. $62 billion wiped from corporate treasury holders. MicroStrategy model questioned. Further downside if Fed hikes.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Favor defensive sectors: utilities, healthcare, consumer staples
defensive_moves.rationale: Tech selloff and rate hike fears favor defensive rotation. Healthcare (AstraZeneca, GLP-1) offers growth with defensive characteristics. Utilities benefit from stable cash flows. Consumer staples resilient to inflation.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Monitor and potentially reduce exposure to media stocks (Paramount, CBS)
defensive_moves.rationale: Paramount deal faces EU and California regulatory hurdles. CBS News controversies (Bari Weiss, Scott Pelley firing). Media sector under pressure from streaming disruption and regulatory uncertainty.

market_outlook.short_term: 1-3 month outlook: Bearish with high volatility. The AI-led tech selloff may continue as Fed rate hike expectations solidify. Bitcoin likely to test lower levels. Oil prices remain elevated due to geopolitical risks. Emerging markets under pressure from strong dollar. Defensive rotation into utilities, healthcare, and cash is prudent. The jobs report reinforces 'higher for longer' rates narrative, weighing on growth stocks.
market_outlook.long_term: 6-12 month outlook: Cautiously bullish. AI infrastructure spending cycle remains intact, providing a floor for semiconductor and tech hardware names. SpaceX IPO could reignite risk appetite. Fed likely to pause after one or two hikes if inflation moderates. Homebuilders and real estate benefit from demographic demand. Healthcare innovation (AI, GLP-1) offers growth. Geopolitical risks may persist but are unlikely to escalate into full-blown conflict. Dollar strength may peak as global growth stabilizes.
key_catalysts: US CPI and PCE inflation data for May/June
Anthropic IPO and AI regulation developments
OPEC+ meeting and oil supply decisions
China stimulus measures and trade talks
watch_list: Fed funds futures and rate hike probability
Nasdaq 100 and S&P 500 technical levels (support at 12,500 and 5,000 respectively)
Oil prices (WTI support at $70, resistance at $85)
Retail investor sentiment surveys and margin debt levels
Lululemon and other consumer discretionary earnings revisions
US screwworm outbreak impact on cattle and agriculture stocks
